rationale: BTN3A1 regulates phosphoantigen sensing by Vgamma9Vdelta2 T cells and modulates NK-cell receptor signaling in the leukemia niche; AML cells may overexpress BTN3A1 to subvert NK surveillance.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct reports linking BTN3A1 to NK-cell inhibition specifically in AML; role inferred from phosphoantigen biology and AML blast expression data.

rationale: GPR174 is a Gs-coupled orphan GPCR that raises intracellular cAMP in lymphocytes; cAMP potently suppresses NK-cell degranulation and IFN-gamma production.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct AML+NK inhibitory reports found; cAMP-raising function inferred from GPCR signaling literature.

rationale: KLRE1 encodes a niche inhibitory lectin-like receptor that may heterodimerize with KLRD1 to form inhibitory complexes on NK cells recognizing ligands on AML blasts.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct reports in AML; inhibitory role inferred from KLR family structure and CD94 pairing.

rationale: KLRB1 (CD161) interacts with LLT1 expressed on AML cells to deliver inhibitory signals; the CD161-LLT1 axis suppresses NK cytotoxicity in myeloid malignancy contexts.
pubmed_result_summary: Documented inhibitory role via LLT1 interaction; specific AML+NK suppression context is underexplored.

rationale: ADGRE1 (F4/80 homolog) mediates suppressive adhesion interactions in the AML bone marrow niche; its expression at the NK-blast interface may impair NK motility and activation.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct AML+NK reports; inhibitory adhesion role inferred from bone marrow niche biology.

rationale: KLRA10 is a human Ly49-family ortholog that recognizes MHC class I on AML blasts to deliver inhibitory signals; understudied in human NK biology compared to mouse Ly49.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct human AML reports; inhibitory MHC-I recognition inferred from Ly49 family biology.

rationale: KLRB1C encodes a human ortholog of mouse NK1.1; its ligand interactions on AML blasts may modulate NK activation thresholds in the leukemia bone marrow.
pubmed_result_summary: Limited human NK data; inhibitory role inferred from mouse NK1.1 biology.

rationale: KLRA7 is the Ly49G2 human ortholog involved in self-recognition via MHC-I; AML blasts expressing elevated HLA may exploit KLRA7 to evade NK killing.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct AML reports; inhibitory function inferred from Ly49 family and MHC-I recognition.

rationale: GPR56 (ADGRG1) is highly expressed on cytotoxic NK cells and regulates their differentiation; its ligand interaction with AML-expressed collagen III may impair NK homing and killing.
pubmed_result_summary: Known role in NK maturation; inhibitory role in AML context inferred from ligand expression data.

rationale: LAIR2 is a soluble decoy receptor that competes with LAIR1 for collagen binding in the bone marrow; elevated LAIR2 in AML may dysregulate the LAIR1 inhibitory axis on NK cells.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct AML+NK reports; soluble decoy mechanism inferred from structural homology with LAIR1.

rationale: SLAMF7 (CRACC/CS1) can switch between activating and inhibitory signaling depending on EAT-2 adaptor availability; in the AML microenvironment lacking EAT-2 this may create net inhibition.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct reports of negative regulation of NK cells in AML; dual-signaling role inferred from adaptor biology.

rationale: IFI44L is an interferon-induced protein that mediates negative feedback in chronic IFN states; sustained IFN exposure in AML may drive IFI44L-dependent NK-cell exhaustion.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct reports; negative feedback role inferred from ISG regulation literature.

rationale: IFIT3 modulates RIG-I signaling; in AML its high expression may divert NK-cell activation signaling toward antiviral instead of cytotoxic programs reducing AML blast killing.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct AML+NK reports; suppressive role inferred from IFN pathway competition.

rationale: IFI6 is an anti-apoptotic ISG that may protect AML blasts from NK-induced apoptosis while suppressing NK-cell IFN-gamma production via negative feedback.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct AML+NK reports; anti-apoptotic protection role inferred from IFI6 function.

rationale: CD300E (LMIR5) is an ITIM-containing inhibitory receptor on myeloid and NK cells; AML-expressed lipid ligands (phosphatidylserine) trigger CD300E to suppress NK degranulation.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct reports in AML+NK context; inhibitory role inferred from CD300 family phospholipid binding.

rationale: SLAMF9 lacks canonical ITAM/ITIM but modulates myeloid-NK crosstalk in the bone marrow; AML-expressed SLAMF9 ligands may create indirect NK suppression.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct reports; indirect suppression role inferred from SLAM family myeloid biology.

rationale: CEACAM1 contains ITIM motifs and mediates homotypic inhibitory interactions between NK cells and CEACAM1-expressing AML blasts suppressing cytotoxicity.
pubmed_result_summary: Reported in NK-cell inhibition contexts; specific AML+NK axis partially characterised.

rationale: CD200R binds CD200 which is overexpressed on AML stem cells; engagement suppresses NK-cell and macrophage activation via downstream cAMP and SHP signaling.
pubmed_result_summary: CD200-CD200R axis reported as immune suppressor in AML; direct NK-cell inhibition partially characterised.

rationale: PILRB is an ITIM-bearing paired immunoglobulin-like receptor; its activation by sialylated ligands on AML blasts delivers inhibitory phosphatase signals to NK cells.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct AML+NK reports; ITIM-mediated inhibition inferred from paired receptor biology.

rationale: CD96 (TACTILE) competes with activating DNAM-1 for PVR/CD155 binding on AML blasts; high AML PVR expression tips the balance toward CD96-mediated NK inhibition.
pubmed_result_summary: Reported as negative regulator in AML NK cells; PVR competition mechanism documented.

rationale: CD160 binds HLA-C and HVEM on AML cells; in chronically stimulated NK cells CD160 delivers inhibitory signals reducing cytokine production and cytotoxicity.
pubmed_result_summary: Limited AML-specific reports; inhibitory role in NK exhaustion inferred from chronic stimulation studies.

rationale: KLRG1 binds E-cadherin and N-cadherin on AML cells to deliver inhibitory signals; marks NK-cell senescence and exhaustion in the AML bone marrow niche.
pubmed_result_summary: Reported as marker of NK exhaustion; cadherin-binding inhibitory mechanism documented.

rationale: LAIR1 binds collagen-rich extracellular matrix in the AML bone marrow; ITIM-mediated SHP-1/2 recruitment strongly suppresses NK-cell activation and degranulation.
pubmed_result_summary: Reported as an inhibitory receptor in tumor microenvironments; collagen-dependent suppression in AML inferred.

rationale: SIGLEC9 binds sialic acids overexpressed on AML blast surfaces; ITIM-mediated SHP-1 recruitment silences NK-cell activating receptor signaling.
pubmed_result_summary: Reported as NK-cell inhibitor binding AML sialic acids; mechanism well characterised.

rationale: CD200R1L is a structural decoy variant of CD200R; it may compete for CD200 binding without transmitting inhibitory signals thereby dysregulating the CD200 checkpoint on NK cells in AML.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct reports; decoy receptor role inferred from structural homology with CD200R.

rationale: KIF9 is a kinesin family member implicated in lytic granule polarisation toward the immunological synapse; its dysregulation in AML-conditioned NK cells may impair granule delivery and target killing.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct reports; granule transport role inferred from kinesin family NK biology.

rationale: A2M-AS1 is an antisense lncRNA to alpha-2-macroglobulin; it may act as a ceRNA sponge for miRNAs that promote NK-cell activation or regulate A2M immunosuppressive protein levels in AML.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct reports; ceRNA and A2M regulatory roles inferred from lncRNA biology.

rationale: PZP (pregnancy zone protein) is an immunosuppressive alpha-macroglobulin; AML cells may secrete PZP to inhibit NK-cell protease activity and suppress cytotoxic granule function.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct reports in AML; immunosuppressive macroglobulin role inferred from pregnancy biology.

rationale: THEMIS raises the activation threshold of lymphocyte receptor signaling; its expression in NK cells may dampen NK responses to AML blasts expressing sub-threshold amounts of activating ligands.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct AML+NK reports; signaling threshold role inferred from T-cell biology.

rationale: SASH1 is a scaffold protein linking TLR signaling to inhibitory pathways; in AML-conditioned NK cells SASH1 may amplify inhibitory signals from pattern recognition receptors in the bone marrow.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct reports; TLR scaffold role inferred from SASH1 signaling biology.

rationale: LUCAT1 is a cancer-associated lncRNA that suppresses interferon signaling; in AML it may reduce NK-cell IFN-gamma production and impair autocrine NK activation loops.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct AML+NK reports; IFN-suppressive role inferred from cancer lncRNA literature.

rationale: PRNCR1 is a prostate cancer-associated lncRNA that may modulate epigenetic programs controlling inhibitory receptor expression on NK cells in the AML microenvironment.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct reports; epigenetic regulatory role inferred from oncogenic lncRNA biology.

rationale: PALB2 mutations or dysregulation in AML may alter NKG2D-ligand expression on blasts paradoxically reducing ligand availability and NK activation despite genomic stress.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct reports; NKG2D-ligand modulation role inferred from DNA damage response biology.

rationale: MS4A2 (FcepsilonRI beta) may modulate receptor clustering and signaling sensitivity on NK cells in the AML bone marrow niche; its expression in myeloid cells could indirectly suppress NK function.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct reports; receptor scaffold role inferred from MS4A family biology.

rationale: UNC13D (Munc13-4) is essential for lytic granule priming and fusion; AML niche factors may downregulate or sequester UNC13D reducing NK-cell degranulation efficiency.
pubmed_result_summary: Known for degranulation; specific downregulation in AML context is underexplored.

rationale: GNAI2 is an inhibitory Gi alpha-subunit coupling to inhibitory GPCRs on NK cells; its activation suppresses adenylate cyclase but dysregulation in AML may paradoxically raise cAMP.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct AML+NK reports; Gi-coupled suppression role inferred from GPCR signaling biology.

rationale: ADCY7 is the predominant adenylate cyclase in hematopoietic cells; prostaglandin E2 and other AML-derived suppressors activate ADCY7 to raise cAMP and inhibit NK degranulation.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct reports; hematopoietic expression and cAMP-raising function inferred from lymphocyte biology.

rationale: ADCY8 is a calcium-stimulated adenylate cyclase; in NK cells activated by AML target cell contact the calcium influx may paradoxically activate ADCY8 raising cAMP and creating a brake.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct reports; calcium-dependent negative feedback inferred from ADCY8 biochemistry.

rationale: KLRC3 (NKG2E) is an understudied NKG2-family member that may compete with activating NKG2D for DAP10 association reducing activating signal amplitude in AML-exposed NK cells.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct AML reports; competitive inhibition inferred from NKG2 family biology.

rationale: ADCY4 is an adenylate cyclase isoform contributing to the suppressive cAMP pool in NK cells; AML microenvironment prostaglandins and adenosine may activate ADCY4 to impair NK function.
pubmed_result_summary: No direct reports; cAMP-producing role inferred from adenylate cyclase family biology.
